item.title: Alpher Instruments Hammerhead 2023 - Black / gold nitro — $4,905.40
item.description:
$4,905.40 · Excellent · Alpher Instruments

Hey folks! Here’s an opportunity to own a bass that is very dear to me! The first ever Alpher Hammerhead!

As i’m sure some of you know I absolutely love this thing. From the moment i plugged it in i knew it was mine.

When building it we picked all the best possible custom options which we feel really work well together. 

it’s light weight, absolutely stacked with tone options and is ridiculously playable. What more could you ask for?

Why am i selling it i hear you ask……

I’m probably gonna ask myself that once it’s gone but as we’re constantly developing new ideas i feel it’s time to push myself into creating the next one! Whatever that may be…..

if you feel you can provide a loving home for what i consider to be one of our finest basses then please get in touch.

  • Hammerhead Custom 4 string
  • 21 fret
  • 34 inch scale
  • One piece Sassafras body
  • One piece quarter sawn Wenge neck
  • Wenge fingerboard with 12 inch radius(39mm brass nut)
  • Brass dot inlays
  • luminlay side dot inlays
  • Jescar EVO gold frets
  • 2 x Sunbear 51p humcuckers
  • Patina brass pickguard
  • Removable wenge thumb rest (using the pickguard screw mounts)
  • Passive master volume, 4 x coil on / off buttons + stellartone bass 10 way tone pot
  • Hipshot vintage bridge 19 spacing with raw brass saddles
  • Hipshot Ultralite lollipop Tuners with D tuner
  • Schaller strap buttons
  • Black + Gold nitro finish inc matching headstock with lacquer checking throughout.
  • Weight: 7lbs 14oz
  • Quality gig bag included
  • £4995 list price
  • Worldwide shipping available

item.title: LightWave (Willcox) Saber Hybrid Edition 5 string 2000s - Black — $999.99
item.description:
$999.99 · Very Good · Ghost Guitarist

The bass is great shape. It’s been very well taken care of over the years. It does have some pick swirls and scratches here and there but nothing crazy deep. It has a couple minor dents in the bottom left wing. The frets have little wear to them plenty life still in them.

Included in the sale is the bass itself, owners manual, and charger for the pickups. Nothing else.

It’s been fully setup to B standard. The bridge has a cool unique pickup/piezo style sound in the bridge that is fully functional and cool. The neck pickup is passive and can still be used if the battery dies.

Message me with any questions. if it’s a concern just ask. I don’t mind.

I pack very well, fast, with the safest route possible. I make sure my packages can take a beating. So you can trust my packing process.

Check out my other listings if you’d like. Thanks for looking.

item.title: Sire 2nd Generation Marcus Miller M7 TBLB Alder 4-String with Fretless Ebony Fingerboard - Transparent Blue Burst — $686.52
item.description:
$686.52 · Excellent · Intrepi's Gear Outlet

Selling my Sire Marcus Miller M7 4st Fretless 2nd Gen, purchased new in 2023.

The bass is in excellent condition, very well maintained, and fully functional.
No issues, no repairs, and no hidden problems.

This instrument has an exceptionally comfortable setup with a super soft and fast neck, making it very easy and inspiring to play. The fretless tone is classic, warm, expressive, and articulate, with great “mwah” and smooth sustain.

The ebony fingerboard is completely straight and in excellent condition with no visible grooves or uneven areas. It comfortably allows very low action and plays cleanly across the neck.

Anyone familiar with fretless instruments knows that fingerboard quality is extremely important, and this is genuinely a very good example of an M7 fretless.

Currently strung with flatwound strings for a smooth playing feel and a traditional fretless character.

Specifications

  • 4-string fretless bass
  • Transparent Blue finish
  • Alder body with flamed maple top
  • Bolt-on maple neck
  • Ebony fingerboard
  • 34” scale
  • Marcus Miller Pure-H humbuckers
  • Marcus Heritage-3 preamp
  • Active/passive mode
  • Black hardware
  • Marcus Miller Big Mass bridge

Condition

  • Excellent overall condition
  • Neck is straight
  • Truss rod works perfectly
  • Electronics fully functional
  • Fingerboard in excellent condition
  • Professionally set up with low action
  • Flatwound strings installed
  • Two small scratches on the rear electronics cover
  • Otherwise only minimal signs of use

A fantastic fretless bass for jazz, fusion, ambient, progressive music, or expressive melodic playing.

Will be packed carefully and securely for shipping.

item.title: 【new】De Gier / Bebop 4 Black 3.835kg #428【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $8,494.29
item.description:
$8,494.29 · Brand New · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

De Gier is a Dutch brand founded in 1996 by Sander De Gier.
He originally focused on creating distinctive and modern bass guitars, but now primarily produces products in a Fender-style.

While the brand was already highly regarded, since the release of the BeBop, a JB-style model, its exceptional quality has kept him completely occupied with production.

Currently, in addition to Fender styles, they also offer unique models incorporating Thunderbird styles.

This model is their huge hit, the BeBop.

Marcus Miller purchased a prototype of this model, and Richard Bona soon followed suit with an order.

It continues to be highly regarded by true musicians like them.

The body is made of ash, and the fretboard is rosewood.

This combination has become increasingly popular in recent years.

De Gier uses various pickups depending on the individual instrument, but this particular instrument features a Lollar pickup.

The bridge is a custom-ordered original style from ETS, and the tuning machines are GOTOH RES-O-LITE.

The color is a chic and beautiful black.

It matches perfectly with the red tortoiseshell pickguard.

While it's basically a passive circuit, it also features a custom Vanderkley Fatboost function.

By slightly boosting the low frequencies and reducing high-frequency loss due to the lower impedance, it gives the impression of boosting both the low and high frequencies simultaneously.

The sound is already complete in passive mode, but simply pushing it a little further seems to give it an even more powerful impact.

This minimalist yet highly effective design is sure to impress all bassists.

The third knob is the passive tone control, but the tone effect can be adjusted using a push-pull switch.

When rolling back the tone, you might want to round off the high frequencies for a warmer sound, or conversely, strongly emphasize the high-mids.

Normally, this would be adjusted by the capacitor choice, but this function allows you to easily select either.

The fourth knob is a rotary switch, which turns Fatboost on and off.

The high strings resonate with a soft touch and rich harmonics, while the low strings maintain the same feel but support the ensemble with a strong fundamental tone—truly the ideal JB.

We promise you'll be impressed.

●SPECS

Ash Body
Maple Neck
Rosewood Board
Dots Inlays
Bone Nut
Lollar Pickups
Black Finish

Delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

Accessories: Gig bag, hex wrench, spec sheet, warranty card

Text: Shunsuke Saga

Inspector: Tei

Product photos: Lee

(Warranty is only valid for purchases made within Japan)

●Price includes VAT & Duties & Shipping Fee
Free Shipping Worldwide. All import duties and taxes are included in the price. There are no hidden fees upon delivery.

If you return a product, you will be responsible for all shipping and customs fees.

Note: All electronic devices, such as effect pedals and amplifiers, are set for use in Japan.
Please be sure to check the voltage and power specifications of your country before purchasing and using.

item.title: Rare Silver Star Long scale Bass Made In Korea 1988 — $537.28
item.description:
$537.28 · Very Good · Joey's gear

Silver Star bass Made In Korea in 1988. Silver Star instruments were manufactured in the Unsung factory, the same factory where other major brands such as Kramer, Fernandes or Dean, among others, are manufactured. This model shares features with the Fernandes Revolver bass from the 80s, solid alder body, maple neck and rosewood fingerboard, black hardware. Its sound is comparable to a high-end bass, very deep and warm, and has better features than other basses, such as weight, only 3.4 kg (7.49 lb), long scale of 875 mm (34.5 inches). In perfect working order and all original, some small scratches on the body, no cracks or repairs. Includes new strings and strong bag.

item.title: Very rare El Cóndor Violin Bass 1970's Made In Japan The Beatles — $955.16
item.description:
$955.16 · Excellent · Joey's gear

Very rare El Cóndor Violin Bass Made In Japan in the 70s imported for the North American market. Ultra rare bass, all solid woods, spruce body, maple neck. High gloss sunburst finish. Very light weight.

All original and sounding amazing, a tone very difficult to find. Very closed to the original Hofner Violin Bass played by Paul McCartney in the Beatles.

It was purchased in North Carolina and It has been in a private collection for decades without being used, it still has the protective plastic from the 70s on the truss rod cover. In spectacular condition, very close to new, minimal signs of use, almost no superficial scratches.

item.title: 【used】Miura Guitars U.S.A. / MB-2 5st 3.97kg #194【Consignment】【GIB Hyogo】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $4,456
item.description:
$4,456 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Miura Guitars is a new brand launched by Mr. Miura, who gained high acclaim for his work at Xotic Guitars.

In addition to high-quality basses, their effects pedals are also highly regarded.

This model is the flagship MB-2.

While the MB series includes some models with lavish and ornate looks, this particular instrument boasts a simple and chic appearance that highlights the natural qualities of the wood.

The body is made of ash, and the neck and fingerboard are made of maple.

This is a classic combination that produces outstanding clarity and a well-defined tone.

The pickups are Aguilar Super Singles.

They are renowned for their clear and powerful sound.

The preamp is the company's famous OBP-3.

It's characterized by its ability to create a very thick and powerful sound that comes through when the EQ is boosted, without sacrificing the naturalness of the original tone.

There is no compromise in the selection of hardware either.

This guitar features Hipshot Ultralight tuning machines and a Hipshot B-Style bridge.

The lightweight tuners reduce neck dive, resulting in excellent body balance while providing tight pitch and rich sustain.

The sound is characterized by a tight, crisp attack and fast response.

The extended mid-to-high frequencies make it well-suited for melodic chord playing.

While the 3-band EQ allows for aggressive playing, the master tone control enables more nuanced and organic sound shaping.

This guitar boasts top-tier playability, capable of meeting all demands in a live setting.

Fret life is approximately 90%.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork are free of noticeable scratches and are in excellent condition.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Master Volume, Balancer, Master Tone (Push/Pull - Active/Passive), Bass, Middle, Treble, Middle Frequency Switch
Nut Width: 47.4mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: Unknown
Country of Manufacture: US

D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians, are used during setup.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings maximize the performance and character of the instrument, so please enjoy them as well.

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

This is a consignment item, so shipping costs may differ from the usual.

Accessories: Hard case, fret guard

Text: Kaigo Kawami

Inspector: Kawami

Product Photos: Lee

GIB 3-Month Warranty Included

The Gibson Rex Brown Signature Thunderbird bass features a mahogany neck and body, gold hardware, a Hipshot bridge, and Mini Clover tuners (with Drop D Xtender). A pair of powerful Rexbucker T-Bird pickups delivers ample output and punch in the low end.

A used Japanese-made Bacchus model has arrived!!

It features an ash body with an ebony fingerboard, and GOTOH parts for the tuning machines and bridge.
Equipped with the original pickups and an Aguilar OBP-2. Controls include 1 Volume, 1 Balance, Treble, Bass, and a Preamp On/Off switch.

item.title: CREWS MANIAC SOUND Jackson 5 Natural (07/13) — $1,892.64
item.description:
$1,892.64 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Cruise Maniac Soundfs long-selling gJackson5,h a flagship high-end brand representing Japan

Featuring a two-piece ash Dinky JB body and a coated fingerboard that extends all the way to the center of the body, this long-selling model has been on the market since the 1990s as the companyfs flagship model.

The golden combination of EMG 40DC pickups and the companyfs BT-S system serves as a textbook example of active bass sound, with each frequency range balanced harmoniously and allowing for control that responds intuitively to the playerfs touch. It also faithfully reproduces the unique resonance of the coated fingerboard.

Due to aging, the finish has developed a cloudy white discoloration.

Body: 2-Piece Ash
Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Ebony / Fretless, with urethane coating (24 frets)
Tuning Machines: Gotoh GB-11
Pickup: EMG 40DC
Preamp: EMG BT-S
Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass; EQ ON/OFF Switch
Aftermarket gig case
Weight: 4.39 kg

item.description:
$1,524.44 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A Fender Japan '62-style Precision Bass; the neck date is unreadable, but based on the serial number, it appears to have been made between 1984 and 1987.
The body is basswood, the neck is maple, the fingerboard is rosewood, and the tortoiseshell-patterned pickguard features a design unique to this period; there is a g55h sticker on the back of the headstock.

The frets have been leveled.
The strap pins may have been replaced.
There is a dent on the headstock, a crack in the finish at the neck joint, and peeling paint on the neck joint plate on the back of the neck.
On the front of the body, next to the bridge, there are screw holes that appear to be from a bridge cover that was once attached, and the paint in the surrounding area is peeling.
There are scratches and dents on the body; the pickguard shows what appear to be sticker residue and playing wear; and the metal parts exhibit rust, tarnish, and pitting.
There are other signs of wear and discoloration, but the overall condition is good.

item.description:
$736.38 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

A used mid-range model from the current BB series has arrived!

This is the Yamaha 700 Series BB734A, a 5-string bass with a bolt-on joint that carries on the legacy of the legendary BB series.

It features a 3-ply alder/maple/alder body, a 5-piece maple/mahogany neck, a rosewood fingerboard, an 863.6mm scale length, a 600R radius, and a 43mm nut. The pickups are YGD V7 P/J (Alnico V) in a PJ configuration, and it includes a volume control, balance control, 3-band EQ, and an active/passive switch.

This 4-string model offers the rich, full-bodied tone characteristic of the classic BB series along with excellent playability. With a through-body bridge, mitered bolted joint, and 3-band EQ, it allows for a wide range of sound shaping and is ideal for home recording.

Although the truss rod is completely loose, the neck is currently in good condition.
There are some minor scuffs, but nothing that detracts from the overall appearance.

The Fujigen 4-String Jazz Bass gJJB6Rh is now in stock!
The J-CLASSIC series is the successor to the J-STD.
Features a basswood body, maple neck, and rosewood fingerboard.
It features Fujigenfs signature Circle Fretting System (C.F.S.).
The C.F.S. improves intonation accuracy at every position and enhances chord cohesion, delivering a natural, rich tone.

item.title: Music Man Stingray Bass, Black, 1979 [B010842] (07/13) — $5,503.50
item.description:
$5,503.50 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

Made in 1979. A Stingray from the Pre-Ernie era has arrived!!

Leo Fender, the creator of gFender,h the original electric bass. Although Leo Fender stepped back from the front lines in 1970, he later launched MusicMan as a new brand in 1972. The StingRay was released in 1976.
This is a StingRay from the gPre-Ernieh era, before the company was acquired by Ernie Ball.

In terms of condition, while the frets and nut have been replaced, the major parts have been preserved in their original state.
The fingerboard was refinished as part of the fret replacement.
The pickup pole pieces are short, and the preamp is the original molded unit from that era.
There are traces of a finger rest that was added to the 4th string side of the pickup.
The mute sponge has melted. The battery snap has been replaced.
There are two marks on the headstock top where a sticker?presumably bearing the previous ownerfs name?was once affixed. These are only visible upon close inspection and are unlikely to be noticeable from a distance. Additionally, there are marks under the pickguard where a sticker appears to have been attached.
The truss rod is on the stiff side and the neck is slightly arched, but it is currently set up to standard string height and presents no issues for playing.

POT DATE: 137-7841 x2, XXX-7839
NECK DATE: JAN 12 1979

item.title: FENDER JAPAN (-2015) AJB-66 (2006?2008) Black [S002890] (07/13) — $1,078.74
item.description:
$1,078.74 · Good · ISHIBASHI MUSIC

We have received a used AJB-66 Black in stock.

It features a basswood body, a maple neck, and a rosewood fingerboard. This combination delivers a well-balanced, easy-to-handle sound and smooth playability.

It features a 2-volume/1-tone control configuration, allowing for versatile tone shaping through the blending of the two pickups.

It is equipped with a gPB SINGLEh pickup in the front and a gJB SINGLEh pickup in the rear. It features a PJ layout, combining a Precision Bass-style front pickup with a Jazz Bass-style rear pickup. It handles a wide range of sounds, from thick, powerful bass to well-defined, clear tones, making it versatile for various genres, including rock and pop.Since the foam padding is firmly adhered to the cavity and the bottom surfaces of both pickups, we were unable to inspect their undersides; however, height adjustment can be performed without issue. Additionally, based on the wiring condition of the control system, it appears the original parts have been retained.

In terms of playability, the neck is in good condition; there are no issues such as buzzing or muffled tones, and it plays comfortably. The truss rod has 60 degrees of adjustment remaining on both sides
. The remaining fret height is approximately 0.8?0.9 mm.

Externally, the guitar shows general signs of use, with several noticeable scratches and dents, but it is still in gplayerfs conditionh and ready for active use.

item.title: G. Gould GGi5 SST 2023 - Metallic Silver — $2,799
item.description:
$2,799 · Very Good · SoCal Bass & Fx

The 2022 G. Gould GGi5 SST (Sweet Spot Turbo) is a premium American-made boutique bass that delivers the legendary performance graphite-neck instruments are known for. Finished in sleek Black, this 5-string features G. Gould's renowned graphite composite neck, providing unmatched stability, incredible sustain, and consistent playability in any climate. The proprietary Sweet Spot Turbo (SST) pickup configuration produces powerful lows, articulate mids, and crystal-clear highs with exceptional note definition and string-to-string balance, making it equally at home on stage or in the studio.

This instrument has been upgraded with a EMG BQC 3-band active preamp featuring a stacked mid-sweep control, allowing you to precisely shape your midrange frequencies while dialing in powerful bass and sparkling treble. The result is an incredibly versatile instrument capable of everything from warm vintage-inspired tones to modern hi-fi clarity with outstanding headroom and low-noise performance. This bass is in excellent used condition, showing only a small cosmetic mark on the lower bout (see image) that does not penetrate the finish or affect performance. For bassists seeking boutique craftsmanship, professional-grade electronics, and the unmistakable feel of a graphite neck, the G. Gould GGi5 SST is an exceptional instrument built to perform for years to come.

Body Wood: Lightweight Alder

Neck: Full-molded graphite/epoxy composite neck

Fretboard: Phenolic fingerboard (24 frets)

Scale Length: 35 inches

Fret Mill: PLEK computerized fret mill for optimum action and playability

Pickups: EMG X active pickups. The SST configuration features a 40DCX at the "sweet spot" (classic M92 punch) and a 40CSX near the bridge (warm growl).

Preamp: EMG BQC System

Controls: Master Volume, Blend, Stacked Bass/Treble, Stacked Sweepable Mid (100Hz to 1KHz sweep)

Power: 18V power system.

Battery: Gotoh Quick-Change battery holder on the rear cavity

Bridge: ABM aluminum bridge, Standard 17.5mm string spacing

Tuners: Gotoh Res-O-Lite

Weight: 9 lbs 2 oz

item.description:

1983 Precision Bass ‘57 Reissue

Herb Gastelum neck


What a bass, with a neck to die for this rare bass was made in the Fullerton plant before the move to Corona. It has the slim neck these were known for. it has the typical ‘80s mod: strap pins and a PJ conversion with Seymour Duncan bass lines.

This Bass also has the prestigious feature of a Herb Gastelum neck. A 57 year veteran of fender who 2 years after this bass became the founding neck guru for the custom shop. His telecasters are legendary and very sought after. This neck is no different.

This neck has what seems like an odd profile, slim but a little wide with a sharp radius from the neck to the fingerboard. Once you play it though…whoah! It just plays super fast, super easy, and just feels like home. I think it is as fast as my jazz bass! to top it off, it was well cared for and is just straight as an arrow. No bows, no twists, and you can absolutely slam the strings For low action. Easily the best neck I’ve ever played.

Only downside for me is the bass lines Pickups. If you love them, great! They’re not my bag though, I always thought they were good, not great. I was going to swap for Lollar or Aguilar myself if I decide to keep.

Couple bumps and one scratch seen in pics. Ships in a non original case or gig bag, your choice.

Note: 10 minimum feedback to purchase.

item.title: Fender American Elite Jazz Bass V — $1,750
item.description:
$1,750 · Very Good · Clifford's Boutique

Up for sale is my Fender American Elite Jazz Bass V in the beautiful Satin Olive Metallic finish with an ebony fingerboard. This has been an incredible bass and has easily become one of my favorite Fender 5-strings I’ve owned. It plays effortlessly with a fast neck, comfortable action, and delivers everything from classic Jazz Bass tones to modern, punchy active sounds.

The American Elite series is one of Fender’s highest-end production models, featuring:

  • 5-string
  • Ebony fingerboard
  • Compound-radius neck
  • 18V active preamp with active/passive switching
  • Noiseless Jazz Bass pickups
  • HiMass bridge
  • Lightweight tuning machines
  • Truss rod adjustment wheel at the neck heel
  • Original Fender Elite molded hard case included (if included)

Condition

Overall the bass is in very good playing condition and functions exactly as it should.

Cosmetically, it does have some finish wear on the lower body edge (pictured). This is purely cosmetic and does not affect playability or performance whatsoever. There are also a few light signs of normal use consistent with a played instrument, but nothing that impacts the functionality of the bass.

I’ve included detailed photos so there are no surprises. The asking price reflects the cosmetic wear.

If you’re looking for one of Fender’s best modern Jazz Basses without paying top dollar for a mint example, this is a fantastic opportunity.

Feel free to reach out with any questions or if you’d like additional photos.
